fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 14-year-old brought by father after expressing that 'death would be easier' following placement on academic probation.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 14-year-old presenting after telling father that death would be easier than facing academic probation and possible retention. Placed on probation yesterday for failing three classes. Endorses passive thoughts that dying would be easier than disappointing family and facing consequences but denies plan, method, or intent. Reports overwhelming shame and fear of father's reaction (father has been supportive). Sleep disrupted, appetite decreased. Has withdrawn from friends out of embarrassment. Previously strong student until this year when began struggling. Enjoys music and plays guitar. Supportive father wants to help. No prior mental health concerns. Currently no medications. (Social Worker Tiffany Adams, LCSW)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Neat, head down, avoids eye contact
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Passive suicidal ideation without plan or intent; catastrophic thinking about failure

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Adjustment disorder with depressed mood related to academic failure; passive suicidal ideation; possible undiagnosed learning disability.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Academic probation; failing three classes; previously strong student suggesting new difficulty; supportive father; social withdrawal; plays guitar; no prior mental health issues.
Assessment_and_Plan: Low risk: passive SI without plan or intent in context of acute academic crisis.
Protective factors: supportive father, music interest, good insight.
Assess for underlying learning difficulties or attention problems causing academic decline.
School evaluation for learning disabilities or ADHD.
Brief counseling for academic stress and catastrophic thinking.
Work with school on academic support plan and probation resolution.
Reframe failure as learning opportunity; reduce shame.
Encourage continued guitar as stress relief.
Follow-up with counselor and school within one week.
Safe for discharge with academic support plan.
fields.Author: Tiffany Adams, LCSW (Social Worker)
